title: Psalms 97
subtitle: The LORD Reigns, Let the Earth Be Glad

sections.header: Verses 1–6 — “Clouds and Thick Darkness Surround Him”

heb.paragraph: "The LORD reigns, let the earth be glad. Clouds and thick darkness (ʿănān) surround him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne" (vv.1–2). The throne’s foundation: not power but righteousness and justice. God’s approach: fire (v.3), lightning (v.4), mountains melt like wax (v.5). The heavens proclaim his righteousness; all peoples see his glory (v.6).

notes.note: "The LORD reigns. Clouds and thick darkness surround him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne." The throne stands on moral categories, not military ones.
"His lightning lights up the world; the earth sees and trembles. Mountains melt like wax before the LORD." The theophany: creation responds to the King’s presence with trembling and melting.

refs.note: All God's angels worshiping the Son quotes the psalm's 'worship him, all you gods.'
Lightning flashing from the throne echoes the psalm's theophanic description.
Sinai's fire, cloud, and trembling earth provides the template for the psalm's theophany.

hist.context: Psalm 97 combines enthronement declaration ('the LORD reigns') with theophanic imagery — clouds, darkness, fire, lightning, melting mountains. This draws on Sinai traditions, presenting YHWH's royal appearing as cosmic upheaval. The command 'worship him, all you gods' subordinates all divine beings to YHWH's sovereignty.

sections.header: Verses 7–12 — “You Who Love the LORD, Hate Evil”

heb.paragraph: "Light (ʾōr) is sown for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart" (v.11). The psalm commands: love the LORD, hate evil (v.10). God guards the faithful and delivers them from the wicked (v.10). Light is planted like seed — not yet visible but growing underground, destined to break through. "Rejoice in the LORD, you who are righteous, and praise his holy name" (v.12).

notes.note: "Let those who love the LORD hate evil, for he guards the lives of his faithful ones." Love for God produces hatred of evil. The two are inseparable. Moral neutrality is not an option.
"Light is sown for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart." Sown: not yet visible. The righteous may be in darkness NOW, but the light is already planted. It will sprout.
"Rejoice in the LORD, you who are righteous, and praise his holy name." The enthronement psalm’s closing command: rejoice and praise. The king reigns; therefore joy.

refs.note: Keeping from idols echoes the psalm's shame on those who worship images.
Hating evil and clinging to good develops the psalm's moral exhortation.
Light dawning for the righteous anticipates Jesus as the light of the world.

hist.context: The contrast between idol-worshipers put to shame and the righteous for whom 'light has dawned' (v.11) reflects wisdom categories. The call to 'hate evil' and love the LORD positions the faithful against idolatry. The closing summons to rejoice 'and praise his holy name' (v.12) unites ethics with worship.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: " Love for God produces hatred of evil…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVAll who worship images are put to shame, those who boast in idols— worship him, all you gods!ESVAll worshipers of images are put to shame, who make their boast in worthless idols; worship him, all you gods!NIVClouds and thick darkness surround him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne.ESVClouds and thick darkness are all around him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne.

rec.text: ""The LORD reigns. Clouds and thick darkness surround him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne." The throne stands on moral categories, not military ones."

rows.label: Light Sown — 97:7–12
rows.text: "Light is SOWN for the righteous." Love God, hate evil. Joy planted underground, destined to sprout.

hebtext.note: "The LORD reigns, let the earth be glad. Clouds and thick darkness (ʿănān) surround him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne" (vv.1–2). The throne’s foundation: not power but righteousness and justice. God’s approach: fire (v.3), lightning (v.4), mountains melt like wax (v.5). The heavens proclaim his righteousness; all peoples see his glory (v.6).

"Light (ʾōr) is sown for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart" (v.11). The psalm commands: love the LORD, hate evil (v.10). God guards the faithful and delivers them from the wicked (v.10). Light is planted like seed — not yet visible but growing underground, destined to break through. "Rejoice in the LORD, you who are righteous, and praise his holy name" (v.12).

themes.note: Sovereignty peaks: the LORD reigns over melting mountains. Holiness: righteousness and justice as throne-foundation. The "light sown" image is remarkable.

prayer_prompt: The LORD reigns; righteousness and justice are the foundation of His throne. Consider asking God about your confidence in His justice — even when outcomes seem unjust.
